hero.subtitle: A full-stack developer who creates fast, accessible, and meticulously designed digital products

bio.title: A Few Words About Me
bio.p1: I’m a full-stack developer who values clean interfaces, performance, and code that’s a pleasure to revisit six months later. Most of my work involves TypeScript, React, and Node, but for tasks where reliability matters more than ‘speed,’ I turn to the .NET ecosystem
bio.p2: When I'm away from the keyboard, I usually read something very smart, tinker with self-hosted services, or study patterns and new technologies

kurisu.tagline: AI agent platform on .NET 10 with an Electron renderer
kurisu.description: An open-source AI agent platform in a single desktop application. .NET 10 contains the agent runtime (MCP, tools, sessions, permissions, hooks) and controls the Electron renderer via a custom bridge. The IPC layer is generated at compile time by the Roslyn source generator, which creates typed TypeScript wrappers from C# method signatures. On the renderer side—shadcn/ui and Vite

react-bits-vue-bits.tagline: Popular open-source component libraries for React and Vue
react-bits-vue-bits.description: Contributor to one of the largest open-source component libraries for React and Vue. Refactored key components (Stack, FadeContent, AnimatedContent): added support for custom scrollers, migrated to GSAP, and implemented safer cleanup. The ferrofluid background on this site is one such component

infiniframe.tagline: A library for creating cross-platform native windows in .NET
infiniframe.description: Active contributor to a cross-platform native window framework for .NET. Worked on the C++ native backend: refactored the unmanaged layer, fixed memory leaks and null-guard issues, and wrote detailed documentation for all four libraries

mpovt.tagline: Corporate showcase website for a major electronics manufacturer
mpovt.description: Multilingual corporate website for «MPOVT» OJSC, developed as a paid commission. A React/Vite SPA with Chakra UI and Tailwind, GSAP for animations, TanStack Query for data, and full i18n support for Belarusian, English, Russian, and Chinese. A light/dark theme system has been implemented

hyprism.tagline: A custom launcher for Hytale with a mod manager and version control
hyprism.description: One of the maintainers of the most popular launcher for Hytale, written in C# for .NET and Electron. I fixed GPU and game instance handling, migrated to a versioning system, created the design and logger, and added support for official authentication.

freaksite.tagline: This site is a platform for typography, motion, and details
freaksite.description: The site you’re currently viewing. A personal space for typography, motion, and small details of interactions. Vite/React/TypeScript, hosted on GitHub Pages

facts.react: My default for product UI: component model, ecosystem, and a codebase I can still read months later
facts.typescript: I reach for it whenever code is shared: safer refactors and clearer contracts between layers
facts.vite: Day-to-day frontend toolchain: fast feedback in dev and lean production builds
facts.tailwind: When I need layout and polish quickly without inventing a CSS architecture mid-sprint
facts.vue: When the team or surface is Vue-native, or a lighter component model fits better than React
facts.electron: Desktop shells where I want a web UI with a native host and real OS integration
facts.gsap: Motion when timing and sequencing matter more than a quick CSS hover
facts.ogl: Small WebGL scenes and backdrops when Three would be overkill
facts.three: Heavier 3D and postprocessing when the scene actually needs the full stack
facts.chakra: Accessible UI primitives when I want speed without designing every control from scratch
facts.framer: Declarative motion that stays in the React tree and composes with layout
facts.i18next: Product copy in multiple languages without hard-coding strings into components
facts.dotnet: Reliability-first backends and long-running services when correctness beats prototype speed
facts.csharp: Main language on the .NET side: runtimes, libraries, and desktop hosts
facts.cplusplus: Native layers where I need control over memory, interop, and OS windowing
facts.nodejs: Tooling, BFF/API, and JS host bridges next to a TypeScript frontend
facts.roslyn: Compile-time codegen so C# contracts stay in sync with TypeScript consumers
facts.sqlite: Local, on-device data when a full server database is unnecessary
facts.mysql: Relational data when structure, joins, and boring predictability matter
facts.mongodb: Document storage when the shape of the data is still moving early on
facts.docker: Isolation and packaging for services and build environments I need to reproduce
facts.webstorm: Daily IDE for multi-package navigation, refactors, and debugging
facts.figma: Interface design, review, and handoff before and during implementation
facts.git: History, branches, and collaboration under every serious codebase
facts.linux: Servers, shells, CI agents, and self-hosted tooling I actually run
facts.cmake: Native multi-platform builds when the C/C++ side has to compile everywhere
facts.githubactions: CI I trust for build, test, and release without babysitting machines

contact.title: Contact Me
contact.intro: I’m always open to interesting projects, questions about technology, or recommendations on what to learn. You can contact me through any of the services below
